Indicator evaluation method: 
 
The TEMPSAL sea temperature extreme variability indicator is based on the computation of the 
99th percentile (that represent approximately the 1% highest values of sea temperature) from 
model results , following the same approach applied by Woodworth and Blackman (2004)  to 
extreme sea levels. This method determines changes in the frequency distribution of the 
measured variables. 
QUID for CMEMS OMI:  
 
TEMPSAL temp extreme variability  
 Ref:    
Date: 
Issue: 
CMEMS-QUID-TEMPSAL-EXTREME-VAR-TEMP 
10 September 2021 
1.2 
Page 3 5 
  
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
All the data used for the computation of the extremes variability indicators ha ve been 
downloaded from Copernicus Marine Environment Monitoring Service (CMEMS). The analysis is 
performed for the sea temperature for three CMEMS regions: Black Sea (BLKSEA), Iberia-Biscay-
Ireland (IBI), Mediterranean Sea (MEDSEA) , and North -West European Shelf (NWS) . For each 
region, the analysis combines information  from both Mult i Year Products (MYP) and Analysis 
Products (NRT). 
 
The following table summarizes the CMEMS products used for the analysis for each region. 
 
Black Sea 
(BLKSEA) 
MYP CMEMS product BLKSEA_REANALYSIS_PHYS_007_004 
PUM: http://marine.copernicus.eu/documents/PUM/CMEMS-BS-PUM-007-004.pdf 
NRT CMEMS product BLKSEA_ANALYSIS_FORECAST_PHYS_007_001  
PUM: http://marine.copernicus.eu/documents/PUM/CMEMS-BS-PUM-007-001.pdf 
Iberia-Biscay-
Ireland 
(IBI) 
MYP CMEMS product IBI_REANALYSIS_PHYS_005_002 
PUM: http://marine.copernicus.eu/documents/PUM/CMEMS-IBI-PUM-005-002.pdf 
NRT CMEMS product IBI_ANALYSIS_FORECAST_PHYS_005_001 
PUM: http://marine.copernicus.eu/documents/PUM/CMEMS-IBI-PUM-005-001.pdf 
Mediterranean 
Sea 
(MEDSEA) 
MYP CMEMS product MEDSEA_REANALYSIS_PHYS_006_004 
PUM: http://marine.copernicus.eu/documents/PUM/CMEMS-MED-PUM-006-004.pdf 
 NRT CMEMS product MEDSEA_ANALYSIS_FORECAST_PHY_006_013  
PUM: http://marine.copernicus.eu/documents/PUM/CMEMS-MED-PUM-006-013.pdf 
North-West 
Shelf Seas 
MYP CMEMS product NWSHELF_MULTIYEAR_PHY_004_009 
PUM: https://catalogue.marine.copernicus.eu/documents/PUM/CMEMS-NWS-PUM-
004-009-011.pdf 
NRT CMEMS product NORTHWESTSHELF_ANALYSIS_FORECAST_PHY_004_013 
PUM: https://catalogue.marine.copernicus.eu/documents/PUM/CMEMS-NWS-PUM-
004-013-014.pdf 
 
The first step of the methodology employed is to compute the 99 th mean annual percentile for 
each MYP product. This is calculated as the mean of the 99 th percentile from each year of data. 
In a second step, the process is repeated for the last year available in the NRT prod uct, and the 
mean data are subtracted, producing the anomaly map of 99th percentile for the last year.

Figure 1.: Iberia-Biscay-Ireland Surface Temperature  extreme variability: Map of the 99 th mean 
percentile computed from the Multi Year Product (left panel) and anomaly of the 99 th percentile in 
2018 computed from the Analysis product (right panel). T ransparent grey areas represent regions 
where anomaly exceeds the climatic standard deviation ( light grey) and twice the climatic standard 
deviation (dark grey).
